reviews.review_description: I wouldn't call it a good movie but it was entertaining. The experience could have been a lot more immersive, it just feels like you are sitting in an  IMAX Dome and not at all like you are part of the action

developer_response.body: Hey Jarhara, thank you for the feedback! The Limit was produced in a format wider than 180 degrees, so the edges of the dome frame and virtual theater are intentional but still give you a fully immersive view without the need to stretch your head and wonder where you should be looking. This lean-back style of VR gives more storytelling control to the directors we're working with. Feel free to send more comments to feedback@thelimitvr.com as we're currently working on future productions in this format and value our fans input!

reviews.review_description: S9 after upgraded to Pie I can't get it install. It keep saying can't install after downloaded. Pls help.

developer_response.body:
Hi kimtatt,

Sorry for the inconvenience, we released an update that should fix your issues. Please download version 1.8 this patches a permission bug present in some S9 hardware.

reviews.review_description:
TL;DR A significant but imperfect effort for 180 cinematic storytelling. 

I prefer 180 to 360 significantly for cinematic work. The 2D version is better as 3D (in this case non-native) halves the resolution.

*****Strong points: 
Good image quality, professional movie craftsmanship from the crew, a solid concept executed at a breakneck pace (no gristle here). The app is attractive and the extra content interests me.
********Weaknesses: 
Inadequate camera stabilization (more could be done in post).
 The gimmick of having the cameraman be the main character who doesn't speak didn't satisfy me. 
Unmotivated POV switch to 3rd person view starting halfway through the piece.
Uninspired story.

All in all it's a bold effort from which we all learn.

Big fan of Norman Redus from WD and Ride and Michelle Rodriguez from F and F so getting this was a given. 
Film shows some interesting potential and is worth checking out. Not 5 * for just 180 and not 360 plus a lot of shaky cámara movement.
Not for VR Rookies
Please make more.

Just finished watching a few minutes ago with just one run no break. I could say Inlike the idea and presentation, how the movie give us new.experience.  It can be a lot better for more budget and production.If this platform success, we may have some other genre like Jaws, Cloverfield, Starwars (which is upcoming on Quest). Support you guys and looking for the next chapter :)
It was a bit pricey for only 18 minutes. I love Michelle Rodriguez and Norman Reedus, but it was just ok.

If you've seen the movie "Hardcore Henry", it's pretty much exactly like that but shorter and in 3D. This is about 18 minutes of action but it's a bit dizzying and blurry on the Oculus Go. It's fun once but I probably won't buy the next chapter if there's one. It's not by any means bad but I think I was expecting more.
Can we make this available on the Oculus quest?
Cinema experience stopped unexpectedly. Not sure why

developer_response.body:
Hi Eassay83

This problem seems to be isolated to some Note 9 models could you tell me which android version you are running so we can pinpoint the problem. Feel free to contact me directly at vrsupport@stxentertainment.com

reviews.review_description: Hot garbage. The only worthwhile part of this is standing beside the glorious Michelle Rodriguez. Story is honking, acting is not up to much and, worst of all, for a VR ‘experience’ you feel completely detached from it, passenger status all the way. Avoid.
Truly unbelievable. Looking forward to more!

I appreciate the attempt the team made here, but the choice for this to be POV with the many action and chase sequences makes this nauseating.    I stuck it out for the duration, but ended up with a headache and am slightly nauseous.  

The effort was definitely there, but I feel enough research wasn’t done during pre-pro.   If they had watched some examples of bad/nauseating POV action VR already out they could have learned some valuable lessons on what to avoid.
I enjoyed it and am very eager to share the experience! Support the content.

reviews.review_title: Needs to be 360 and use proper 3D.
reviews.review_description:
It's hard to explain but what throws me off is the fact it tries to make you feel like your in the video playing a part in the movie but keeps breaking the immersion, for example..

1) Your looking at something and the camera swings fast around unexpectedly 

2) One minute your view is 
head height and next thing your view is from what appears to be your waist height (Am I a person playing a role or a fly on the wall?

3) Black edges!! Seriously? Someone flies over me and I can't watch them land? I'm looking in a mirror and see stuff happening behind me but I can't turn to see it... no no I need to wait for the camera to do that for me...

4) Fake 3D. Some few scenes are fine with this technology but most would look best with stereoscopic. Sometimes it looks a little too fake, especially when it's a VR device and people are used to stereoscopic vision.

Anyway that's my two cents, good but has potential to be great if things were done a little differently.

I can’t wait for these experiences to be in full 360 but for now, Robert Rodriguez doesn’t disappoint with this action packed thrill ride. In the future for the sake of inclusivity, I would have gloved or concealed the person’s distinguishable “features” so the POV of YOU is conveyed as more generic and can be anyone (male, female, Black, White) But it’s not that big of a deal that you’re playing a white male in this experience, I get it as I would imagine the demographic is mostly that right now. Things will change, however.

It's perfect in 180 degrees,  or would be impossible follow the lead character around, maybe story can be improved but Robert Rodriguez made an awesome work on edition and continuity to control spectators eye from many distractions (later can watch the details) Michelle was gorgeous
Not downloading
